items.headline: Study session on zoning and parkland protections
items.summary: City Council will review existing designations, zoning corrections, and state law protections affecting recreational facilities and parkland sites in Cupertino.

items.headline: Fifth Third Bank signage permitted at 250 Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The bank is cleared to install three illuminated LED signs on its downtown commercial building.

items.headline: Sunnyvale to study banning chemical pesticides on city property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will study a policy to eliminate the use of chemical pesticides on all city-owned or leased property. The outcome will determine whether Sunnyvale shifts to alternative pest management methods across parks, facilities, and other municipal land.

items.headline: Sunnyvale Sustainability Commission to review artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will conduct a policy study on artificial turf use in Sunnyvale. This will likely examine environmental and maintenance impacts of synthetic turf on city property.

items.headline: Samsung facility receives $19.9M building permit at N 1st Street
items.summary: A Samsung Technology and Innovation facility at 3655 N 1st St is cleared to build, with a construction budget of nearly $20 million.

items.headline: Demo and rebuild permitted on Sherwood Avenue
items.summary: A complete demo/rebuild of the home at 1194 Sherwood Av is permitted for $484,245, replacing one unit with a new single-family residence.

items.headline: Historic house on Crescent Avenue to make way for townhomes
items.summary: A Resource Alteration Permit was continued to July 1, 2026, for a project at 434 Crescent Avenue that would replace a historic 960-square-foot single-family house with four three-story townhomes in the rear. The Heritage Preservation Commission will review whether the demolition of the existing historic resource is justified by the new development.
